auth_prerequisites: Realistic evaluation requires Google account access and Gemini auth or API enablement.

non_interactive_execution.summary: The public npm package and GitHub install guidance expose an installable CLI entrypoint.
non_interactive_execution.notes: The execution surface is public and installable without private artifacts.

offline_strategy.summary: Help/version fixture capture or a fake binary can cover parser-facing wrapper behavior before account-backed evaluation.
offline_strategy.notes: Recommendation needs wrapper-seam evidence, not live provider interaction.

observable_cli_surface.summary: The repo and package metadata make the terminal CLI surface externally observable.
observable_cli_surface.notes: Public repo and package metadata provide enough observable CLI proof.

redaction_fit.summary: Terminal IO remains text-first and can flow through the repo redaction layer.
redaction_fit.notes: Repo-fit claim is an inference from terminal-oriented CLI behavior.

crate_first_fit.summary: An external CLI install path maps cleanly to the repo wrapper-first onboarding ladder.
crate_first_fit.notes: The integration seam remains an installed external binary, not an embedded runtime.

reproducibility.summary: Public npm distribution makes local setup repeatable, with account auth reserved for later proving.
reproducibility.notes: Account-backed behavior is deferred, but install and wrapper seams are repeatable now.

future_leverage.summary: High upstream adoption and active release channels make Gemini CLI strategically relevant even though it is already onboarded.
future_leverage.notes: The candidate remains strategically important despite registry-based ineligibility in this run.

blocked_steps: Provider-backed evaluation still needs Google account auth and any applicable API or billing enablement.
normalized_caveats: Public install and release evidence is reproducible now; account-tier behavior belongs to later proving.
